scope.setting: exact finite-dimensional observer-region channels and finite-dimensional OA-QEC algebras
scope.operational_input: relative-entropy response/defect under physical observer-region channels
scope.bridge_input: relative-entropy response for the composed observer-to-observer bridge channel
scope.example_probe_family: full-rank qubit pairs (I +/- rP)/2 for P in {X,Y,Z}, r=0.5
scope.claim_boundary: finite-dimensional exact theorem package; not continuum ER=EPR, de Sitter, dS/CFT, or type-III algebra

core_claim: In exact finite dimension, preserved relative entropy on an algebra's state space is equivalent to observer-region reconstructability of that algebra; the bridge-transferred observer algebra is the largest algebra preserved by the composed bridge channel.

known_theorem_substrate.claim: Petz sufficiency and finite-dimensional OAQEC provide the recovery implication.
Relative-entropy preservation underlies entanglement-wedge reconstruction arguments.
Perfectly preserved information structures of quantum processes have finite matrix-algebra structure.

new_programmatic_contribution.claim: Packages relative-entropy preservation as an intrinsic observer-bridge diagnostic, not a labeled logical-probe protocol.
Records finite examples where static entropy shadows agree but relative-entropy response separates quantum, classical, and null observer bridges.
Frames Goal 18's stabilizer local-screen theorem as an exact Pauli special case of finite-dimensional relative-entropy response.
Identifies approximate/noisy relative-entropy observer-bridge recovery as the actual major-unlock frontier.
Records the known state-level universal-recovery fidelity lower bound from relative-entropy defect without claiming a full noisy algebra theorem.

exact_theorem_style_claims.claim: A finite-dimensional observer algebra is reconstructable on R iff the region channel preserves relative entropy for all full-rank state pairs in that algebra's state space.
The bridge-transferred observer algebra is the largest subalgebra whose relative entropy is preserved by the composed observer-to-observer channel.
The diagnostic input is distinguishability preservation under physical channels, not a supplied multiplication table or labeled logical basis.

finite_certificate_evidence.status: state-level bound recorded, algebra-uniform noisy theorem open

proof_obligations.claim: Emit the relative-entropy observer-bridge theorem certificate.
Run focused regression for static-shadow collision and response separation.

limitations: Exact finite-dimensional theorem package only. Approximate/noisy observer-bridge reconstruction and type-III/continuum gravity remain open.
